feedback.text: It has always been in the eyes of every consumer: Smartphones. It is common on computers that you can easily install any software, wherever and whatever you want. Consumers have been denied this right from the outset on smartphones. Apple has been going through here since the first iPhone and gets a lot of headwinds. Google saw the potential of this gap and introduced so-called ‘sideloading’ with Android. In fact, it should only be called ‘installing’, because more than it is not. Every consumer can thus install apps as they want. This open approach is nowadays: many independent developers want or even cannot afford to verify Google (25$ once) or Apple (99$ per year!). In 2025, Google will see a turnaround: Sideloading/installing from any app through installation packages will be removed. But in the same breath, computers will be rendered obsolete and replaced by smartphones and tablets. We are currently in an extremely difficult and challenging development in the near future, which needs to be countered. On behalf of all consumers and small and medium-sized enterprises, I am in favour of sideloading being handled as a normal installation process on the basis of a European law and made mandatory. As in the near past with the USB-C obligation, Europe can be an important sign of a more open future.
